name: abelian
description: Adversarial collaboration framework (Kahneman-style applied to LLM dispatch) for deep, innovative, long-horizon iteration with tractable doc and testable metric. v3.0 unification: ONE skill, ONE loop, ONE discipline — every configured peer (default 2) proposes AND attacks every other peer's proposals; mutual inspiration; mechanism-converge termination. 18 INVARIANTS rules harden against fabrication, drift, compaction, mission drift, cross-layer evidence confusion, fuzzy-program-contract, fuzzy-mission, and propose/counter discipline asymmetry. Rule #18 (NEW): peer must be innovative + grounded when proposing, strictly verification-oriented when countering attacks. Goal-authoring is a stage of the unified loop (auto-detected from --mission flag), not a separate mode. Prior mode lexicon (--mode=unilateral / co-research / --adversary=X) deprecated; --peers=<config> selects peer-tool pair.
